title: Operating System
description: Practice year-wise MCQs from 2018 to 2026 with explanations and answer review.

questions.prompt: Which scheduling algorithm may starve long jobs?
questions.explanation: Shortest Job First can favor short jobs and delay longer ones indefinitely.

questions.prompt: A process state after it has completed execution is:
questions.explanation: A terminated process has finished execution and is no longer runnable.

questions.prompt: The kernel of an operating system is responsible for:
questions.explanation: The kernel manages CPU, memory, devices, and process scheduling.

options: A process waits indefinitely for resources
questions.answer: A process waits indefinitely for resources
questions.explanation: Deadlock is a state where processes are stuck waiting for each other.

questions.prompt: Which memory management technique allows non-contiguous allocation?
questions.explanation: Paging divides memory into fixed-size pages and allows non-contiguous allocation.
Round Robin allocates each process a fixed time quantum in cyclic order.
Semaphores are synchronization primitives used to coordinate concurrent processes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a virtual memory concept?
questions.explanation: Swapping moves processes or pages between main memory and disk to extend available memory.

questions.prompt: The primary goal of scheduling is to:
questions.explanation: Scheduling aims to make efficient use of CPU time and provide responsive service.
